title: GTA 6 is borrowing one of Forza Horizon 6’s best features
body: Grand Theft Auto 6 looks set to give car enthusiasts another reason to explore Leonida, as a newly revealed activity lets players hunt down abandoned vehicles and restore them to their former glory. According to Rockstar’s website, the Ultimate edition will feature a “Classic Car Collection” special commission, which tasks players with tracking down abandoned classic cars and unfinished project vehicles scattered across the map before revitalizing them. The description reads: “Track down a variety of abandoned classic and work-in-progress project cars and revitalize them to their former glory in this special commission from eccentric collector and local fixer, Wyman.” While Rockstar has yet to fully detail how the activity works, the concept will feel familiar to racing game fans. The feature bears a strong resemblance to Forza Horizon 6’s Barn Finds, which send players across the map searching for hidden vehicles before restoring them and adding them to their collections. Forza’s Barn Finds have long been one of the franchise’s most popular side activities, rewarding exploration with rare and unique cars that can’t be obtained elsewhere. GTA 6 news keeps coming ahead of launch The Classic Car Collection reveal arrives amid a steady stream of GTA 6 announcements as Rockstar prepares for the game’s release later this year. The company confirmed pricing for multiple editions of the game on June 24, including a premium Ultimate Edition that offers additional content and bonuses. Rockstar has also faced criticism over plans to lock certain in-game stores and businesses behind the more expensive $100 version. Elsewhere, Sony has promoted GTA 6’s performance on PlayStation 5, claiming players can expect significantly faster loading times compared to previous entries in the series. Related The game has also generated controversy outside of gaming circles, with some retailers reportedly refusing to stock physical copies due to the absence of a disc in the box, while one country has already moved to ban GTA 6 over concerns surrounding its violent content. With pricing, platform features, and edition-exclusive content already dominating discussion, the discovery of a new classic car restoration activity gives fans another look at what they’ll actually be doing once they finally arrive in Leonida.

title: GTA 6 is already banned in one country for “encouraging murder”
body: Grand Theft Auto 6 is set to launch on November 19, but the game has already been banned in one country for its graphic content. On June 24, Rockstar revealed new info about pre-order bonuses and the game’s price, with the Ultimate Edition locking in-game stores and other goodies behind its $99 price tag. The announcements also saw Sony detail how the PS5 will be the “best” console to play GTA 6 on, with the sprawling state of Leonida featuring “near instant” load times. Unfortunately, fans in one country won’t be able to get their hands on GTA 6. Not because a physical copy won’t contain an actual disc, but because it’s been banned. Has GTA 6 been banned in several countries? Rumors explained Shortly after pre-orders were announced, rumors spread that the game had been banned in many countries, citing info on Sony’s GTA 6 page about how players can get a free month of GTA+ by pre-ordering the game. The page states that this will apply to “all PlayStation country stores, excluding Bahrain, China, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, Russian Federation, Taiwan.” However, this only applies to the free GTA+, and GTA 6 isn’t banned in the Gulf just yet. Meanwhile, Grand Theft Auto had been banned in Thailand since 2008 after a player, supposedly influenced by the game, killed a taxi driver. For GTA 6, a Thai PlayStation store page with pre-order info exists, suggesting the game has been approved. So far, it seems like the only country to have completely banned GTA 6 is Tajikistan. In 2024, the country banned both GTA and Counter-Strike for ‘inciting crime’ and encouraging “murder, theft and violence.” Related Russia took aim at GTA 6 over male strippers Earlier this year, Mikhail Ivanov, deputy chairman of the World Russian People’s Council called Grand Theft Auto a “dangerous tool of influence.” Of note, he took issue with one of the game’s leaked inclusions: male strippers. “The creators of GTA VI deliberately go to include destructive and vulgar content in their product, completely unacceptable to the moral health of society,” he blasted. “This includes the planned inclusion of scenes of male striptease in the game, which is a direct and cynical violation of basic moral norms and traditional spiritual values.” The Russian went on to demand that Rockstar release a sanitized version in the country, but it doesn’t seem like the devs have given in and the title hasn’t been banned. Although GTA 6 has yet to be officially rated, Sony does note that games in the series t

title: Why GTA 6 Hype Keeps Growing Even When Rockstar Says Nothing
body: Rockstar Games has released almost nothing about Grand Theft Auto VI. So far, we've only gotten the first and second trailer, a handful of screenshots, a dedicated website, pricing, and a cover art reveal. This is very nearly the entire official output for the most anticipated game in history and it it has fuelled years of non-stop coverage. Is it working? The pre-order numbers suggest yes. However, that doesn't make it any less strange. Most companies flood you with information to build excitement. Rockstar does the opposite. It says very little, very rarely, and somehow that scarcity generates more attention than any marketing blitz ever could. The Four Stages of the GTA 6 Hype Cycle The repeating cycle that turns a single piece of Rockstar content into months of sustained attention. Rockstar generates enormous coverage even when it does nothing at all. The silence itself becomes content. When there is no news, the community and the press manufacture it, from a countdown to the next possible reveal, to theories built on a blurry background detail, to speculation about why Rockstar has gone quiet. The absence of information is part of the GTA 6 hype cycle, which is not standard. A typical big game does a steady drumbeat, gameplay reveals, developer diaries, previews, interviews, hands-on events, all carefully spaced out to keep you interested. What Rockstar is doing is manufactured and artificial scarcity and this is the reason why it works so well. When something is rare, we want it more. Rockstar has understood this for over a decade, and it has the one thing that makes scarcity work, a product so wanted that people will obsess over crumbs. Most studios cannot do this. If a middling game went silent for a year, everyone would simply forget it. Rockstar can go quiet precisely because of the the incessant demand. We have to admit. We're part of feeding that hype machine. Whenever Rockstar drops a trailer, we analyze it. When it goes silent, we cover the theories and the leaks. So when we describe a machine that turns press and fans into an unpaid marketing department, we are describing ourselves too. We are a working part of it. This isn't so much a confession as the actual point we're trying to make. Rockstar's marketing runs on all of us, the outlets chasing traffic, the YouTubers chasing views, the fans chasing the next detail, and insiders looking for the next scoop. The genuine excitement that Rockstar barely has to lift a finger to sustain powers ev

title: GTA 6 Price: Every Edition and What You Get
body: Grand Theft Auto VI costs $79.99 for the Standard Edition and $99.99 for the Ultimate Edition in the United States. Both prices were confirmed by Rockstar Games ahead of pre-orders opening on June 25. The Standard Edition is the complete game. It includes the full story, the entire map, and access to the online mode when that goes live. The Ultimate Edition adds a bundle of vehicles, cosmetics, customisation venues, and some exclusive side content. We have broken down exactly what that extra $20 buys you item by item if you want the full list before deciding. One thing worth knowing before you commit either way is you aren't locked into your choice. Standard Edition buyers have the option to purchase the Ultimate Edition Upgrade separately after redeeming their code. If you are undecided, buying Standard and upgrading later costs you nothing extra, so there is no penalty for waiting to see whether the bonus content appeals to you once the game is out. As for the regional pricing, Rockstar doesn't simply convert the dollar price into your currency. It sets prices region by region, adjusting for local economies, currency strength, taxes, and what buyers in that market can realistically pay. The spread is wide, running more than $75 between the cheapest and most expensive markets. GTA 6 Price by Region Confirmed storefront prices for Grand Theft Auto VI in the largest markets, sourced from official PlayStation Store and Xbox Store listings. The United States, despite the $79.99 headline everyone quotes, is not the expensive end of the scale. Buyers in the United Kingdom, the eurozone, and Australia are paying more in real terms, and Israel sits nearly $30 above the American price. Then again, cheapest in dollars is not the same as cheapest in life either. Around $63 in India represents a far larger share of a typical income than $79.99 does in the United States, so the markets that look like bargains on this table are often the ones where the game is hardest to afford. As for the GTA 6 price, the standard for big-budget console games have stood at $69.99 since roughly 2020, when Take-Two Interactive became the first major publisher to move above the previous $59.99 standard. Before the pre-orders opened, some analysts predicted a $100 base price and others believe Rockstar went wrong by pricing GTA 6 too cheap, so $79.99 landed above the expected figure and below the worst-case one, making neither shareholders and Grand Theft Auto fans happy. Take-Two CEO Str

title: GTA 6 Release Date, Platforms, and What Is Locked In
body: Grand Theft Auto VI releases on November 19, 2026, on PlayStation 5, PlayStation 5 Pro, and Xbox Series X and S. That's it. That's the answer. That's when the next Grand Theft Auto will come out on current-gen platforms barring an unlikely delay. If you want to watch the clock run down for some reason,our countdown page tracks it to the second. Confirmed GTA 6 Platforms Every platform Grand Theft Auto VI has been confirmed for at launch, and the ones that have not been dated. There is no launch version for the previous console generation. If you own a PlayStation 4 or an Xbox One, you will need newer hardware to play this game. As for playing the next GTA on the PC, Rockstar Games has yet to confirm it. Historically the studio has brought its games to PC after a console-first period, so it's coming. Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick has also talked about it before. For now though, anyone quoting you a specific PC release date is speculating or guess. GTA 6 Release Date Changes Every publicly stated release window for Grand Theft Auto VI and how it changed. GTA 6 giveaway Win a full copy of GTA 6 Ultimate Edition.We're giving away full copies of GTA 6 Ultimate Edition in several draws through launch. Enter with your email, then join in once on the site. One entry per person. Prize A full copy of GTA 6 Ultimate Edition Email first, then one site action during the draw window. The official GTA 6 release date has changed three times, including an specified date as revealed by Zelnick. However, there's no reason to believe it will change one more time. The current release date is embedded in Take-Two's financial guidance for the 2027 fiscal year, reaffirmed in fillings and on the record. The company has told its shareholders and financial regulators to expect a specific, enormous amount of revenue in a fiscal year that ends on March 31, 2027, and the only product capable of producing that figure is GTA 6. Is a GTA 6 delay still possible? Yes, but for that to happen, Take-Two and Rockstar will have to open themselves up to financial and legal consequences beyond just disappointing fans. Key GTA 6 Dates Confirmed dates in the lead-up to launch, and what happens on each. A note on the GTA 6 physical release. The boxed version is a download code in a case rather than a disc, so buying physically does not get you a playable disc even though retailers will eventually run out of copies to sell. It gets you a code you redeem. Plan accordingly if you were countin

title: GTA 6's $80 Price Marks the End of the $60 Standard Game
body: The $60 video game lasted fifteen years. The $70 video game lasted five. Grand Theft Auto VI isn't the first game to sell its standard edition at $79.99, but will most likely be the game that will set the floor for every big-budget game that follows it, deserved or not. AAA Game Price Tiers Adjusted for Inflation The table tracks the standard launch price of big-budget console games across four console generations, how long each price tier held before the next increase, and what each price is worth in July 2026 dollars. The $60 standard, set around 2005 with the Xbox 360 and PlayStation 3 generation, held for fifteen years. Then, in August 2020, Take-Two Interactive priced NBA 2K21 at $70 and became the first publisher to break the $60 barrier for the new console generation. Now the next Grand Theft Auto sits at $79.99, and every publisher watching knows that the psychological wall is no longer there. Even worse is that some analysts believe Rockstar Games should've sold GTA VI for more. To be fair, Rockstar and Take-Two didn't just sell GTA VI for $80 just because they could. Development costs have risen in the past decade. When Grand Theft Auto V reportedly cost around $265 million to make in 2013, and GTA VI reportedly cost north of a billion, you cannot sustain that curve on a price that has barely moved. Besides, inflation is a real thing. A $60 game in 2005 would cost around $103 in today's money. Adjusted for inflation, the $80 GTA 6 standard edition is the cheapest AAA launch price in three decades. The industry hasn't been raising prices so much as slowly failing to keep up with them. However, GTA 6 is not just an $80 game. It is an $80 standard edition positioned as the complete game, sitting under a $100 Ultimate Edition that locks actual gameplay content behind the upgrade. It's a meaningful change from how deluxe editions used to work, and it's a template every other publisher is studying right now. For years, premium and deluxe editions sold you cosmetic fluff, a skin pack, some in-game cash, a soundtrack nobody listened to. You never felt like you were missing actual game. GTA VI changes this by putting playable content on the other side of the $20 wall. If it works, and the pre-order numbers suggest it will work spectacularly, then we should expect every major publisher, from Electronic Arts to Ubisoft to Sony Interactive Entertainment, to adopt some version of the same two-tier structure within a year or two. When the biggest game ever pro

title: 'Please Play When GTA 6 Comes Out': AC Black Flag Resynced Will Add New Features to Keep You Aboard
body: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced launched only recently, but already, its director is promising more to come for the highly acclaimed remake. Speaking in a video interview, Ubisoft Singapore's Richard Knight is asked about post-launch support for the open world pirate adventure, and while he doesn't make any official announcements, he shares one or two tidbits about what the team is planning. Before that, though, he pleads with fans to stick with the game in a post-GTA 6 world. "I mean, I can’t say anything officially, but you know, it doesn’t feel like a crazy bet," he says. "I mean, we definitely need to support the game... We need to, you know, fix bugs. Sorry for anyone running into bugs. They hurt. They’re painful for us, too. And we do want to add some quality-of-life stuff. So, I mean, it’s a pretty reasonable guess. Also, please play us when GTA 6 comes out." It seems the main thing the studio is working on currently is New Game Plus mode, though Knight can't say precisely when it'll be released. A common complaint from players is about the blowpipe and how it appears on Edward's back when equipped. Knight says they're working on making it so you can hide the weapon so it doesn't show up on your character. The devs are also working on adding the catch ledge mechanic as seen in Assassin's Creed Shadows, and could potentially add side objectives to missions, though this isn't set in stone. It seems Knight and the team are committed to improving Resynced in the coming months, then, and he'd really like it if you stick with the game once GTA 6 is out.

title: GTA VI Price Concerns Grow as PS Store Moves to Local Currencies
body: GTA VI Price Concerns Grow as PS Store Moves to Local Currencies GTA VI Price Concerns Grow as PS Store Moves to Local Currencies PlayStation Store currency changes could widen the pricing gap between digital and retail GTA VI copies. Highlights - Sony will shift the PlayStation Store to local currencies in Mexico, Honduras, and Nicaragua on Aug 20, 2026. - Mexico’s wallet conversion rate has raised digital pricing concerns, though Sony has not confirmed a GTA VI price increase. - Retail competition and pre-order discounts could make boxed GTA VI copies more competitive than PlayStation Store purchases. GTA VI buyers in parts of Latin America could soon have another reason to compare digital and retail prices. Sony will move the PlayStation Store to local currencies in Honduras, Mexico, and Nicaragua on Aug 20, 2026. The change comes three months before GTA VI launches on Nov 19, 2026. In Mexico, existing wallet funds will convert at 20.50 pesos (~$1.17 USD) per U.S. dollar (USD). The market rate cited in the provided information is around 17.50 (~$1) pesos. The gap has raised concerns about digital game pricing after the transition, though value-added tax (VAT) could factor into the final cost. However, Sony has not confirmed that GTA VI will receive a price increase. Physical Retail Could Give GTA VI Buyers More Options Retail stores could gain an advantage if digital prices become less competitive. Unlike the PlayStation Store, multiple retailers can compete through discounts and pre-order offers. Boxed GTA VI copies have already received price reductions in France. Rockstar is also using a code-in-box format for some GTA VI editions. Buyers would still download the game, but purchasing through retailers could provide more pricing options than buying directly from Sony. The change follows calls for better regional pricing in Latin America. PlayStation Store prices in some markets have historically been listed in USD. Steam, by comparison, offers regional pricing that can better reflect local purchasing power. Sony has also tested personalized discounts, though they are not available to everyone. PlayStation-focused X account @NotiPlay_ reported that Store maintenance will begin two days before the change. It also reported that promotions will remain unavailable until Sep 23, and USD gift cards will stay valid. PS3 Store access will end in the three markets after maintenance. Sony is also expected to expand local pricing to more countries. Brazil is unaf

title: Why GTA 6 Launches on Consoles First: A Veteran Producer Explains
body: Consoles first, PC later. Here’s the real reason behind the GTA 6 schedule. Why GTA 6 Launches on Consoles First: A Veteran Producer Explains A former GTA 5 producer reveals how console optimization, hardware scaling challenges, and strategic resource allocation dictate the staggered GTA 6 release schedule. Highlights - Standardized hardware allows Rockstar to maximize performance without the burden of diverse PC configurations. - The labor-intensive process of optimizing code for lower-tier hardware necessitates a delayed PC launch. - Rockstar prioritizes engineering resources on flagship console releases over the high costs of immediate PC porting. Grand Theft Auto 6 is slated to arrive this November for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, but PC gamers will be left waiting on the sidelines at launch. While frustrated fans have long suspected that Rockstar Games staggers its releases purely as a business strategy to get players to buy multiple copies of the game, former GTA 5 producer John Ricchio has shed light on the real story. Speaking on the Kiwi Talks podcast, Ricchio, who worked at the legendary studio from 2003 to 2014, confirmed that the delayed PC release is primarily a technical necessity born from the sheer complexity of hardware optimization, rather than just a marketing tactic. Working within the closed ecosystems of home consoles gives developers a massive advantage when trying to push hardware to its absolute breaking point. Because console specifications and thermal limits are standardized across every living room, Rockstar's engineering teams can dedicate all their resources toward maximizing performance without having to account for endless PC configurations. Ricchio emphasized that having a single, known target allows developers to write highly efficient code without worrying about whether a player is trying to run the game on a ten-year-old graphics card. The biggest technical hurdle in bringing a massive open-world title to PC lies in the labor-intensive process of scaling a game down to run on lower-tier consumer hardware. According to Ricchio, shrinking a game's performance footprint is exponentially harder than extending it. While having extra computing power on a high-end rig easily allows developers to turn up shadow resolutions and render objects further out in the distance, optimizing for weaker hardware requires writing entirely new code to stop rendering objects early or manually decimating character meshes. The veteran prod

title: GTA VI Pre-Order Record Signals Potential $5.2B First Week
body: GTA VI Pre-Order Record Signals Potential $5.2B First Week GTA VI Pre-Order Record Signals Potential $5.2B First Week GTA VI's digital pre-orders are estimated at $260M globally as Rockstar prepares for its wider launch campaign. Highlights - Newzoo projects GTA VI could generate between $3.25B and $5.2B in first-week revenue. - Global digital pre-orders are estimated at roughly $260M, marking Newzoo's largest observed opening. - GTA VI launches Nov 19, 2026, with Rockstar's full marketing push and Trailer 3 still ahead. Grand Theft Auto VI (GTA VI) might generate between $3.25B USD and $5.2B in its first week, according to Newzoo. At the upper end, the forecast would put its launch revenue at nearly four times GTA V's roughly $1.15B first-week total. GTA VI has already recorded a strong start in digital pre-orders. Newzoo's Game Performance Monitor tracked $180M in spending across the U.S. and five major European markets. The firm estimates the global total at roughly $260M. Newzoo’s management consultant, Ronan Patrick, told Insider Gaming that it was "the largest opening Newzoo has observed." The firm also rejected claims that GTA VI had already made $1B from pre-orders as "absurd." GTA VI Could Surpass Earlier Revenue Estimates Rockstar Games has yet to begin its full marketing push. Trailer 3 is also still pending, leaving room for demand to grow before launch. Newzoo's projection factors in sales of the $79.99 standard edition and the $99.99 Ultimate Edition. The early pace could also put GTA VI ahead of a previous estimate by Konvoy General Partner, Josh Chapman, who projected $7.6B in revenue during its first 60 days. However, GTA V set another benchmark when it crossed $1B in sales within three days of its 2013 launch. GTA VI's early performance suggests the gap between the two releases could widen as its launch approaches. GTA VI is scheduled for Nov 19, 2026, on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. Reports have claimed that PlayStation pre-orders are outpacing Xbox by six to one. Lower marketing visibility on Xbox was cited as one factor, although Microsoft disputed the claim. The game's boxed version will contain a download code instead of a physical disc. This keeps distribution digital and could reduce Rockstar's physical production costs. GTA VI was also reportedly removed from some PlayStation marketing amid criticism of Sony's wider digital strategy. The change does not appear to have affected early pre-orders. With months remaining before release, th

title: 'GTA 6' Fans Want Smarter, Dynamic Traffic to Make Leonida Feel More Realistic
body: "Grand Theft 6" fans are currently debating how much traffic Rockstar Games should add to the streets of Leonida. The discussion is focused on finding the right balance between realism and enjoyable gameplay, with many players comparing their expectations with the traffic system in "GTA 5." A busy road network can make an open-world game feel more alive. However, too many vehicles could also make traveling across the map frustrating. Players could face constant traffic jams during missions, while police chases might become unnecessarily difficult if crowded streets prevent a clean getaway. 'GTA 6' Fans Want Dynamic Traffic Patterns One of the most popular ideas among the community is a dynamic traffic system that changes depending on the time of day and the player's location. Instead of keeping traffic levels relatively consistent throughout the map, "GTA 6" could simulate different patterns based on how a real city operates. Downtown areas, for example, could become heavily congested during morning and afternoon rush hours as commuters travel to and from work. At night, those same streets could become noticeably quieter. This would allow players to travel more quickly while creating a stronger sense that Leonida is an active world that changes throughout the day. Dynamic traffic could also affect how players approach missions. A player planning a heist might choose a specific time based on expected road conditions. Starting an operation at night could make escaping easier, while a busy rush hour could provide more opportunities to blend into traffic or create confusion during a getaway. Different Vehicles Could Become More Useful A more realistic traffic system could also make "GTA 6's" wide range of transportation options more valuable. When roads become crowded, players could turn to motorcycles, boats, or helicopters. Motorcycles could be particularly useful because they can navigate between vehicles and potentially escape congested areas more easily than larger cars. Boats could provide alternative escape routes through waterways, while helicopters could allow players to bypass traffic entirely. This could encourage players to think more strategically about transportation rather than relying on cars for every journey. Could Take-Two Technology Improve 'GTA 6' Traffic? Some fans have connected these discussions to patents associated with Take-Two Interactive that describe technology for improving the behavior and navigation of autonomous characters and

title: Former Rockstar Producer Expects Massive 'GTA 6' World to Be 'Monstrous' in Size
body: "Grand Theft Auto 6" is expected to deliver Rockstar Games' most ambitious open world yet, according to a former developer who worked on several of the studio's biggest titles. Ahead of its controversial release, many believe "GTA 6" will not only feature Rockstar's largest map but also its most immersive and detailed environment to date. Set in the fictional state of Leonida, the next installment in the "Grand Theft Auto" series promises a richer open-world experience with dynamic locations, improved realism, and more opportunities for exploration. While Rockstar Games has yet to reveal the full map, early trailers and official details suggest the studio is pushing the boundaries of what players can expect from an open-world game. Former Rockstar Producer Predicts a Bigger 'GTA 6' Map Former Rockstar Games producer John Riccitiello (John Ricchio) recently discussed the scale of "GTA 6" in an interview, pointing to Rockstar's long history of expanding its game worlds with each new release. (Note: if referring to the former Rockstar producer, ensure the name is correctly cited, as former EA CEO John Riccitiello is incorrect; verify the original source, as some reports identify the former producer differently.) According to the former developer, "Grand Theft Auto 5" showed a "monstrous" size and detail compared to "Grand Theft Auto 4," while the "Red Dead Redemption" series also demonstrated the studio's commitment to creating increasingly immersive environments. Based on Rockstar's track record, he expects "GTA 6" to feature either the franchise's largest map or an unprecedented level of environmental detail that makes the world feel more alive than ever before. Leonida Could Become Rockstar's Most Immersive Setting Although Rockstar has only revealed portions of Leonida, the fictional state already appears to offer a diverse range of locations inspired by modern-day Florida. Rather than focusing solely on map size, Rockstar seems to be emphasizing world density and realism. Players can expect bustling cities, smaller towns, beaches, swamps, highways, and countless interactive locations designed to encourage exploration beyond the main story. GTA 6's Massive Budget Reflects Its Ambition The scale of "GTA 6" is also reflected in reports surrounding its development cost, according to Gaming Bolt. While Rockstar Games has not officially confirmed the game's budget, multiple industry reports have claimed that development and marketing expenses could exceed $1 b

title: 2026 Game Release Schedule: GTA 6, Halo Campaign Evolved, and Forza Horizon 6 Headline the Year
body: GameSpot has released its updated list of confirmed game release dates for 2026 across various platforms, including PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X and S, Switch 2, Switch, and mobile.  The list, compiled by writer Gabe Gurwin, includes titles with fixed launch dates as well as those with a broader release window for the year. It is expected to change as publishers announce or delay their plans.  The most prominent entry is  Grand Theft Auto 6 , scheduled for release on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox Series X and S, though there is a possibility of a delay, according to GameSpot.  This schedule can be useful for gamers planning hardware upgrades, subscription renewals, or managing their game backlog around specific franchises. Below are some of the notable releases per month, based on the dates provided by GameSpot.  Early 2026: Trails, Nioh 3, and Resident Evil Requiem   January  kicked off with The Legend of Heroes: Trails Beyond the Horizon, releasing on January 15 across PS5, PS4, Switch 2, Switch, and PC. Following that, Code Vein 2 is coming out on January 30 for PS5, Xbox Series X and S, and PC.   February  starts with a busy schedule. Nioh 3 is launching as a PS5 exclusive on February 6. On February 5, Dragon Quest VII Reimagined arrives on PS5, Xbox Series X and S, Switch 2, Switch, and PC. High on Life 2 and Reanimal are both set for release on February 13.  The month concludes with Resident Evil Requiem, which will be available on February 27 across PS5, Xbox Series X and S, Switch 2, and PC.  March: Crimson Desert, Death Stranding 2 on PC, and Monster Hunter Stories 3  March is one of the busiest months on the gaming calendar. Crimson Desert is set to launch on March 19 for PS5, Xbox Series X and S, and PC, marking its return after years of delays.  The same day, Death Stranding 2: On The Beach will be released on PC. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection is scheduled for March 13 across PS5, Xbox Series X and S, and Switch 2.  The remake of Fatal Frame II: Crimson Butterfly is planned for March 12, and Bungie's extraction shooter Marathon is launching on March 5.   Nintendo's Switch 2  edition of Super Mario Bros. Wonder, along with the Meetup in Ballabel Park expansion, is set to arrive on March 26. WWE 2K26 and MLB The Show 26 will be released on March 13 and March 17 respectively.  April: Pragmata, Diablo IV Expansion, and Saros  Pragmata, Capcom's highly anticipated sci-fi game that experienced delays, is scheduled to launch on April

title: GTA 6 Hacker Arion Kurtaj Moved From Secure Hospital to Prison Ahead of November 2026 Retrial
body:
Arion Kurtaj, the hacker linked to the Lapsus$ group and responsible for the September 2022  GTA 6  leak, has been moved from indefinite hospitalization at a secure clinic to a regular prison.  This change comes ahead of a retrial scheduled for November 2026, according to BBC journalist Joe Tidy. Kurtaj was initially sentenced in December 2023 to indefinite detention in a medical facility after being deemed unfit for trial due to a diagnosis of acute autism. The upcoming retrial will occur in the same month that GTA 6 is expected to be released, on November 19, 2026.  Details about the case remain largely undisclosed, with authorities remaining tight-lipped about the preparations for the retrial.  What Happened in the 2022 GTA 6 Leak and Kurtaj’s Sentencing  Kurtaj's 2022 hacking incident was unprecedented in scale. He released 90 clips from GTA 6 along with the game's source code on a fan forum. The circumstances surrounding the hack made it particularly notable:  
 Kurtaj was out on bail after hacking Nvidia at the time and was under police protection at a Travelodge hotel. 
 He used only an Amazon Firestick, the hotel's TV, and a cell phone to infiltrate Rockstar's systems. 
 The attack succeeded despite his surveillance and monitoring status. 
  The leak attracted widespread attention and led Rockstar Games to confirm that GTA 6 was in development, ahead of its planned official reveal.  In December 2023, a jury found Kurtaj guilty of the hacks. He was sentenced to imprisonment in a secure hospital under conditions that could keep him there for life or until he was deemed fit to stand trial again.  The unusual sentence reflected concerns about Kurtaj's mental health and behavior in custody. A mental health assessment cited by the BBC noted that Kurtaj continued to express the intention to return to cybercrime as soon as possible and was highly motivated. Authorities also reported dozens of incidents involving injury or property damage during his time in police custody.  Move From Secure Hospital to Prison and November 2026 Retrial Timing  BBC's Joe Tidy confirmed on Bluesky that Kurtaj has been moved from a secure hospital to a regular prison. This move suggests that authorities now believe he is medically fit to stand trial. Tidy is trying to find out more about the case but has described police responses as "like getting blood out of a stone."  The retrial is scheduled for November 2026. Kurtaj's specific charges and potential sentence have not been p

title: GTA 6 fans are overpaying eBay scalpers for pre-orders despite no shortage
body: Grand Theft Auto 6 fans are already paying above retail price to pre-order the game through eBay, despite there being no shortage of copies to buy directly. GTA 6 pre-orders officially opened on June 25, with Rockstar confirming the Standard Edition will cost $79.99 and the Ultimate Edition will cost $99.99. The long-awaited sequel is set to launch on November 19, 2026, for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. Rockstar also confirmed that digital pre-loading will begin on November 12. Even the physical edition of GTA 6 is not a traditional disc release, as the boxed version only contains a download code for the game, meaning there is no real scarcity around discs or stock in the usual sense. GTA 6 pre-orders are already selling above retail price on eBay Despite that, multiple sold eBay listings show buyers paying well above retail price for GTA 6 pre-orders. One sold listing for a PS5/Xbox Series X|S pre-order went for $138.20, with an additional $17.03 delivery charge. Another PS5 presale listing from the US sold for $120, plus $9.53 delivery. Other sold listings show the standard PS5 edition going for $87.90, $93.22 with $20.47 delivery, and $101.19, all above the confirmed $79.99 retail price. One Ultimate Edition listing sold for $101.19, slightly above the $99.99 retail price. The listings appear to be for pre-orders rather than rare physical stock, making the markup especially unusual. GTA 6 can still be pre-ordered through official digital storefronts, and boxed copies will not include a disc when they arrive in stores. Rockstar’s physical edition is scheduled to be available from November 12 to support pre-loading, but it will only include a code that can be redeemed for the digital download. Rockstar wrote in the pre-order details for physical editions, “a disk will not be included in the box.” Related The situation is another sign of just how massive demand is for GTA 6, even when buyers are effectively paying extra for access to something that is still widely available at retail price.
